title: Russia Security Desk
public_claim: The Russia Desk is a two-page analytical workbench: a contemporary security desk for regime security, military adaptation, influence operations, Ukraine, NATO, and strategic decision-making; and a history page for the longer imperial, Soviet, post-Soviet, and European-order inheritance that gives present judgment depth.
distinctive_mechanism: The mechanism is source-lane to judgment-lane conversion. Internal workbench files, Kindle/Routledge/source library source families, journal and transcript manifests, marker packs, visual dossiers, exact-source queues, and source lineage bridges are separated by role.
use_value: It links regional knowledge to analytic method by preserving source lineage, strategic frame, adversary logic, and claim limits for later Russia/security work.

flagship_proof_builds.name: Russia Desk analytic-variable proof
flagship_proof_builds.claim: The desk turns Russia material into analytic variables rather than generic commentary.
flagship_proof_builds.input: source lane, regime-security evidence, military evidence, influence evidence, and historical inheritance
flagship_proof_builds.operation: separate variables, test alternatives, bound confidence, and state public claim limits
flagship_proof_builds.expected_output: structured Russia assessment with source lane, variable, alternative, and confidence boundary
flagship_proof_builds.verification_check: show one claim moving through source lane, variable assignment, alternative review, and public claim restraint

component_proofs.claim_supported: separates source families and exact-source candidates before strategic judgment; the step records the decision path so the reader can see how the project moves from input to governed output.
component_proofs.input: books, journals, transcripts, maps, lecture notes, local pages, source markers, and visual dossiers
component_proofs.expected_output: admitted Russia source lane as a reviewable artifact
component_proofs.current_evidence: Expected proof artifact: admitted Russia source lane as a reviewable artifact
component_proofs.verification_check: Trace one Russia claim through Russia Source Lane; confirm source lane, analytic variable, confidence boundary, and policy-facing output.

claim_ceiling: The page may claim proof-structured components and visible verification targets. It may not claim external validation, production deployment, or completed empirical findings unless the project-specific record states those facts.
